//: The Store write side (Phase 5). POST /store/plugins/{key}/enable and POST /store/plugins/{key}/disable toggle a plugin's activation with no tenant branching. Responses: 200 with the plugin's refreshed Store entry (same shape as an item in /store/plugins — dependency health is recomputed, since disabling one plugin can flip another's 'satisfied'); 404 if no plugin has that key; 409 if the plugin is in a Failed state (fix and reload it first). Disabling is idempotent (a second disable is a no-op). In the first-party modular monolith, enabling returns a plugin straight to 'Started'; the out-of-process Store runtime will load and start the assembly on enable.
